title: EdgarTools Platform Agent Guide
scope_summary: Operational reference guide for AI agents working in the edgartools-platform repository, covering architecture, tooling rules, AWS/Snowflake deployment flows, warehouse CLI commands, and safety rules. Provides authoritative guidance on AWS-only deployment paths, runtime settings, and idempotency constraints.
locked_decisions: AWS is the only active deployment path; non-AWS paths must not be added or revived without explicit user request
AWS Terraform must not create runnable ECS task definitions, Step Functions state machines, schedules, workload commands, image rollouts, or runtime secret values
Use uv for all Python dependency management; bare pip is prohibited
Loader default behavior skips already-captured SEC files; --force is required for repair
Ownership parser import must use edgar.ownership.Ownership.from_xml(...); do not change without checking edgartools changelog
Do not broaden IAM policies casually; keep runner roles service-assumed and scoped
Do not destroy prod bronze storage without explicit operator request and reviewed migration plan
key_requirements: EDGAR_IDENTITY must include an email address or the runtime rejects the command
WAREHOUSE_RUNTIME_MODE must be bronze_capture or infrastructure_validation
Gold-affecting commands require SERVING_EXPORT_ROOT (SNOWFLAKE_EXPORT_ROOT accepted as fallback)
Snowflake Enterprise or higher is required for dynamic tables
SnowCLI connection must exist before running deploy-snowflake-stack.sh
snowflake_export_root_url must include trailing slash on snowflake_exports/
edgartools>=5.29.0 is required (install from PyPI, not vendored)
key_constraints: Do not commit local secrets, .tfvars with live values, generated Terraform state, or application JSON with sensitive values
Do not put image digests, workflow rollout, schedules, or EDGAR identity values into AWS Terraform inputs
Large files (runtime.py, silver.py, gold.py) must be read in chunks before editing
Use Colima on macOS for local Docker; do not introduce other container build/runtime stacks
S3 backend state locking uses use_lockfile=true; no DynamoDB lock table required
prod uses protected storage; bronze bucket has prevent_destroy=true
Runner roles must be service-assumed; do not create runner access keys
